{
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "devices": [
    {
      "deviceKey": "ThingRobot",
      "displayName": "Aimee (Mining Robot)",
      "logicDescriptions": {
        "Mode": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "0-6",
          "description": "Controls Aimee's autonomous behavior:\n• 0 = None (idle, brakes applied)\n• 1 = Follow (follows nearest player)\n• 2 = MoveToTarget (direct path to TargetX/Y/Z)\n• 3 = Roam (wander and auto-mine ore)\n• 4 = Unload (deposit ore to nearby input)\n• 5 = PathToTarget (pathfind to TargetX/Y/Z)\n• 6 = StorageFull (auto-set when full)"
        },
        "TargetX": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "World coords (m)",
          "description": "Target X coordinate for MoveToTarget (Mode 2) and PathToTarget (Mode 5). Set this along with TargetY and TargetZ to direct Aimee to a specific location."
        },
        "TargetY": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "World coords (m)",
          "description": "Target Y coordinate (altitude). Used with MoveToTarget and PathToTarget modes. Y is vertical height in the world."
        },
        "TargetZ": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "World coords (m)",
          "description": "Target Z coordinate for navigation. Combined with TargetX and TargetY to form the destination point."
        },
        "PositionX": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "World coords (m)",
          "description": "Aimee's current X position in world coordinates. Use for tracking location or calculating distance to targets."
        },
        "PositionY": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "World coords (m)",
          "description": "Aimee's current Y position (altitude). Useful for determining if robot is above or below ground level."
        },
        "PositionZ": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "World coords (m)",
          "description": "Aimee's current Z position in world coordinates."
        },
        "VelocityMagnitude": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0+ m/s",
          "description": "Aimee's total speed. Returns 0 when stationary. Use to detect if robot is stuck (< 0.1 for extended time)."
        },
        "VelocityRelativeX": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "m/s",
          "description": "Velocity along Aimee's local X axis (sideways movement relative to robot facing)."
        },
        "VelocityRelativeY": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "m/s",
          "description": "Velocity along Aimee's local Y axis (vertical movement relative to robot)."
        },
        "VelocityRelativeZ": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "m/s",
          "description": "Velocity along Aimee's local Z axis (forward/backward relative to robot facing)."
        },
        "Orientation": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-360°",
          "description": "Aimee's heading/yaw orientation in degrees. 0 = North, 90 = East, 180 = South, 270 = West."
        },
        "MineablesInQueue": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "0+",
          "description": "Currently broken & deprecated. DO NOT USE"
        },
        "MineablesInVicinity": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "0+",
          "description": "Number of minable ore deposits within 32-block range. Use to check if area has ore before sending Aimee to mine."
        },
        "ForwardX": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "-1 to 1",
          "description": "X component of Aimee's forward direction vector. Part of the unit vector indicating which way the robot is facing."
        },
        "ForwardY": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "-1 to 1",
          "description": "Y component of forward direction vector. Typically near 0 unless robot is tilted."
        },
        "ForwardZ": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "-1 to 1",
          "description": "Z component of forward direction vector."
        },
        "Power": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Returns 1 if Aimee has battery power, 0 if battery is dead or missing."
        },
        "On": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Power state. Write 1 to turn on, 0 to turn off. Must have battery to operate."
        },
        "Error": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Returns 1 if Aimee has an error (no chip, chip compilation error, or stuck). Check this to diagnose problems."
        },
        "Lock": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Lock state. Write 1 to lock (prevents player interaction), 0 to unlock."
        },
        "Activate": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Activation state. Similar to On but used for triggering specific actions."
        }
      },
      "slotDescriptions": {
        "Battery": {
          "slotType": "Battery",
          "description": "Powers Aimee's movement and mining operations. Use a large battery for extended autonomous operation."
        },
        "ProgrammableChip": {
          "slotType": "ProgrammableChip",
          "description": "IC10 chip slot for custom automation scripts. Program navigation, mining patterns, and unload logic here."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "deviceKey": "ThingStructureAdvancedFurnace",
      "displayName": "Advanced Furnace",
      "logicDescriptions": {
        "Mode": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "0-3",
          "description": "Furnace operation mode:\n• 0 = Idle (no processing)\n• 1 = Smelt (process ores → ingots)\n• 2 = Alloy (combine ingots, needs upgrade)\n• 3 = Incinerate (destroy items, generate heat)"
        },
        "SettingInput": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-10 L/tick",
          "description": "Input flow rate in liters per tick. Controls how much gas/liquid is drawn FROM the input pipe network INTO the furnace chamber. Higher = faster input."
        },
        "SettingOutput": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-10 L/tick",
          "description": "Output flow rate in liters per tick. Controls how much gas is pushed FROM the furnace chamber TO the output pipe network. Higher = faster exhaust."
        },
        "Setting": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-10 L/tick",
          "description": "Same as SettingOutput. Target output flow rate for gas exhaust from the furnace."
        },
        "Temperature": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0+ K",
          "description": "Current internal temperature of the furnace in Kelvin. Must reach recipe temperature to smelt. ~1500K for most ores."
        },
        "Pressure": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0+ kPa",
          "description": "Internal pressure of furnace chamber. High pressure can damage the furnace. Vent excess gas to control."
        },
        "Power": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Returns 1 if furnace is powered, 0 if no power. Requires cable connection."
        },
        "On": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Power state. Write 1 to turn on, 0 to turn off."
        },
        "Error": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Returns 1 if furnace has an error (overpressure, overtemp, or no fuel)."
        },
        "Activate": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Activation state. Returns 1 if furnace is actively processing."
        },
        "Combustion": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-100%",
          "description": "Current combustion rate. Higher values = faster heating. Depends on fuel mixture and pressure."
        },
        "RequiredPower": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0+ W",
          "description": "Power currently required by the furnace. Scales with SettingInput + SettingOutput usage."
        },
        "Lock": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Lock state. Write 1 to prevent player access to controls."
        },
        "RatioOxygen": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Ratio of oxygen in furnace atmosphere. Needed for combustion."
        },
        "RatioVolatiles": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Ratio of volatiles (fuel) in furnace. Burns with oxygen to generate heat."
        },
        "TotalMoles": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0+ mol",
          "description": "Total moles of gas inside the furnace chamber."
        }
      },
      "slotDescriptions": {
        "Import": {
          "slotType": "Import",
          "description": "Input slot for raw ores and items to be processed. Insert ores here for smelting or items for incineration."
        },
        "Export": {
          "slotType": "Export",
          "description": "Output slot where finished ingots appear after smelting. Use a stacker or chute to automate collection."
        }
      },
      "versionDescriptions": {
        "Tier One": {
          "description": "Basic furnace. Lower efficiency but cheaper to build. Good for early game smelting."
        },
        "Tier Two": {
          "description": "Improved efficiency and speed. Reduced material and energy costs per smelt operation."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "deviceKey": "ThingStructureSatelliteDish",
      "displayName": "Satellite Dish (Medium)",
      "logicDescriptions": {
        "Mode": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "N/A",
          "description": "Satellite dish does not use Mode. Control via Horizontal/Vertical angles and Setting (power)."
        },
        "Setting": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "0-5000 W",
          "description": "Transmission power in watts. Higher power = longer range but more electricity used. Must exceed trader's MinimumWattsToContact."
        },
        "Horizontal": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-360°",
          "description": "Horizontal angle (azimuth) in degrees. Controls dish rotation. 0° = reference direction, rotates clockwise."
        },
        "Vertical": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-90°",
          "description": "Vertical angle (elevation) in degrees. 0° = horizontal, 90° = straight up. Point at sky to find traders."
        },
        "HorizontalRatio": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Horizontal position as ratio. 0 = 0°, 0.5 = 180°, 1 = 360°. Alternative to degree-based control."
        },
        "VerticalRatio": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Vertical position as ratio. 0 = 0° (horizontal), 1 = 90° (straight up)."
        },
        "SignalStrength": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-180°",
          "description": "Angular offset from strongest contact in degrees. Lower = better alignment. -1 if no signal or resolving."
        },
        "SignalID": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "ReferenceId",
          "description": "Reference ID of the strongest detected trader contact. Returns -1 if no contact found. Use with db instruction."
        },
        "MinimumWattsToContact": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0+ W",
          "description": "Minimum watts required to establish contact with strongest signal. Your Setting must exceed this value."
        },
        "WattsReachingContact": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0+ W",
          "description": "Actual watts reaching the contact after distance/alignment losses. Must exceed MinimumWattsToContact for communication."
        },
        "InterrogationProgress": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "0-1 or -1",
          "description": "Contact interrogation progress. 0-1 during interrogation, 1 when complete, -1 if no contact. Interrogation reveals trader inventory."
        },
        "ContactTypeId": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "Hash",
          "description": "Hash ID of the trader type at strongest contact. Identifies what kind of trader (Merchant, etc)."
        },
        "Idle": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Returns 1 if dish motors have stopped moving, 0 if still rotating to target position."
        },
        "TargetPadIndex": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "0+",
          "description": "Index of landing pad to direct incoming traders to. Set via IC10 to route traders to specific pads."
        },
        "BestContactFilter": {
          "dataType": "Integer",
          "range": "ReferenceId or -1",
          "description": "Filter to lock onto specific trader by ReferenceId. Set to -1 to scan all contacts. Set to SignalID to lock onto current."
        },
        "SizeX": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "Grid units",
          "description": "Required landing pad X dimension for current contact. -1 if unknown or resolving."
        },
        "SizeZ": {
          "dataType": "Float",
          "range": "Grid units",
          "description": "Required landing pad Z dimension for current contact. Check your pad is big enough!"
        },
        "Power": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Returns 1 if dish is powered, 0 otherwise."
        },
        "On": {
          "dataType": "Boolean",
          "range": "0-1",
          "description": "Power state. Write 1 to turn on, 0 to turn off. Dish only scans when on."
        }
      },
      "memoryDescriptions": {
        "WriteTraderData": {
          "opCode": "WriteTraderData",
          "parameters": "address, value",
          "description": "Writes trading parameters to the satellite dish memory. Used to configure automated trading operations with contacted merchants."
        },
        "StrongestContactIdHash": {
          "opCode": "StrongestContactIdHash",
          "parameters": "none",
          "description": "Returns the ID hash of the strongest signal contact detected by the dish. Use to identify which trader you're communicating with."
        },
        "StrongestContactMetaData": {
          "opCode": "StrongestContactMetaData",
          "parameters": "index",
          "description": "Returns metadata about the strongest contact at the specified index. Contains trader type and capability information."
        },
        "StrongestContactSignalData": {
          "opCode": "StrongestContactSignalData",
          "parameters": "index",
          "description": "Returns signal data for the strongest contact. Includes signal strength, distance, and alignment information."
        },
        "WriteTraderBuyData": {
          "opCode": "WriteTraderBuyData",
          "parameters": "slot, prefabHash, quantity",
          "description": "Configures what items to BUY from the trader. Specify the item's prefab hash and desired quantity."
        },
        "WriteTraderSellData": {
          "opCode": "WriteTraderSellData",
          "parameters": "slot, prefabHash, quantity",
          "description": "Configures what items to SELL to the trader. Specify the item's prefab hash and quantity to sell."
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "genericDescriptions": {
    "logic": {
      "Power": "Returns 1 if device is powered correctly, 0 if not. Most devices require cable connection to power grid.",
      "On": "Current power state. 1 = on, 0 = off. Write to toggle device power via IC10.",
      "Error": "Returns 1 if device is in error state, 0 otherwise. Check device-specific conditions for causes.",
      "Lock": "Lock state. 1 = locked (prevents player interaction), 0 = unlocked. Useful for automation security.",
      "Activate": "Activation state. Usually indicates if device is actively running its primary function.",
      "Open": "Returns 1 if device is open, 0 if closed. Applies to doors, hatches, and containers.",
      "Temperature": "Current temperature reading in Kelvin. 273K = 0°C, 293K = 20°C room temperature.",
      "Pressure": "Current pressure reading in kPa. 101.325 kPa = 1 atmosphere at sea level.",
      "Ratio": "A 0-1 ratio value. Context-specific meaning varies by device.",
      "Quantity": "Total quantity/count on the device. Usually item count in storage.",
      "Charge": "Current electrical charge stored in the device. Typically for batteries.",
      "RequiredPower": "Power draw in watts when device is operating. Idle devices may draw less.",
      "ReferenceId": "Unique identifier for this device instance. Use with db instruction for batch operations.",
      "PrefabHash": "Hash code identifying the device type. Same for all instances of this device.",
      "LineNumber": "Current line being executed on programmable devices. Useful for debugging IC10 scripts.",
      "Horizontal": "Horizontal angle or position. Usually 0-360 degrees for rotational devices.",
      "Vertical": "Vertical angle or position. Usually 0-90 degrees for elevation controls.",
      "Setting": "A configurable value that controls device behavior. Meaning varies by device type."
    },
    "slots": {
      "Battery": "Slot for battery cells. Powers the device when not connected to grid or for portable operation.",
      "ProgrammableChip": "IC10 chip slot. Insert programmed chips to automate device behavior.",
      "GasCanister": "Slot for gas canisters. Provides or stores atmospheric gases.",
      "GasFilter": "Filter slot that restricts gas flow to specific gas types.",
      "SoundCartridge": "Audio cartridge slot for speakers and alarms.",
      "DataDisk": "Data storage disk slot for programs or saved data.",
      "Import": "Input slot for items entering the device for processing.",
      "Export": "Output slot where processed items exit the device.",
      "Ore": "Slot specifically for ore items in mining/processing equipment."
    },
    "versions": {
      "Tier One": "Basic version. Standard efficiency and material costs. Good for early game or backup systems.",
      "Tier Two": "Improved version. Better efficiency, faster operation, reduced resource consumption."
    },
    "memory": {
      "StackPointer": "Returns or sets the current stack pointer position in device memory.",
      "ExecuteRecipe": "Triggers execution of a crafting/processing recipe in the device.",
      "JumpToAddress": "Jumps execution to a specific memory address.",
      "WaitUntilNextValid": "Pauses execution until the next valid condition is met.",
      "JumpIfNextInvalid": "Jumps to address if next instruction would be invalid.",
      "DeviceSetLock": "Sets the lock state of the device programmatically."
    }
  }
}

```
- prompt:

```
Review the Phase 1 implementation of StationpediaPlus expansion.

## Phase Objective
Expand the data model and JSON schema to support all 7 category types for Stationpedia tooltips: Logic, LogicSlot, Mode, Connection (all using SPDALogic), Slots (SPDASlot), Versions (SPDAVersion), and Memory/Instructions (SPDAGeneric).

## Files Modified
1. `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\StationpediaPlus.cs`
2. `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\descriptions.json`

## Changes Made
1. **Data Classes Updated**:
   - `DescriptionsRoot` - Changed `genericDescriptions` from `Dictionary<string, string>` to `GenericDescriptionsData`
   - `DeviceDescriptions` - Added `slotDescriptions`, `versionDescriptions`, `memoryDescriptions` dictionaries
   - Added new classes: `SlotDescription`, `VersionDescription`, `MemoryDescription`
   - Added `GenericDescriptionsData` class with sub-dictionaries for logic, slots, versions, memory

2. **Helper Methods Added**:
   - `GetSlotDescription(deviceKey, slotName)` - Returns slot description with fallback to generic
   - `GetVersionDescription(deviceKey, versionName)` - Returns version description with fallback
   - `GetMemoryDescription(deviceKey, instructionName)` - Returns memory instruction description with fallback

3. **JSON Schema Updated**:
   - Version bumped to 2.0.0
   - `genericDescriptions` restructured with `logic`, `slots`, `versions`, `memory` sub-objects
   - Added example `slotDescriptions` to ThingRobot
   - Added example `versionDescriptions` to ThingStructureAdvancedFurnace
   - Added example `memoryDescriptions` to ThingStructureSatelliteDish

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Code compiles without errors
- [ ] JSON loads correctly with new schema
- [ ] All new data classes are properly serializable
- [ ] Helper methods correctly fall back to generic descriptions
- [ ] Existing Logic/LogicSlot/Mode/Connection tooltips still work

## Review Tasks
1. Verify code correctness and adherence to C# best practices
2. Check for potential null reference issues
3. Verify JSON schema is well-formed
4. Check that existing functionality is not broken
5. Identify any issues or improvements needed

Return your review in this format:
- **Status**: APPROVED / NEEDS_REVISION / FAILED
- **Summary**: Brief summary of findings
- **Issues**: List any issues found (numbered)
- **Recommendations**: List any recommended improvements

## Phase 1 Complete: Expand Data Model & JSON Schema

Expanded the C# data classes and JSON schema to support all 7 category types for tooltips: Logic, LogicSlot, Mode, Connection, Slots, Versions, and Memory/Instructions.

- StationpediaPlus/mod/descriptions.json

- `GetSlotDescription()` - New helper for slot tooltips with generic fallback
- `GetVersionDescription()` - New helper for version tooltips with generic fallback
- `GetMemoryDescription()` - New helper for memory instruction tooltips with generic fallback
- `LoadDescriptions()` - Updated to load new GenericDescriptionsData structure

**Classes created/changed:**
- `SlotDescription` - New class for slot type descriptions
- `VersionDescription` - New class for structure version descriptions
- `MemoryDescription` - New class for memory instruction/opcode descriptions
- `GenericDescriptionsData` - New class with sub-dictionaries (logic, slots, versions, memory)
- `DeviceDescriptions` - Added slotDescriptions, versionDescriptions, memoryDescriptions dictionaries

```
- Add example entries for slots, versions, and memory instructions
```

You are implementing Phase 2 of the StationpediaPlus expansion plan.

## Objective
Attach tooltip components to Slots, Versions, and Memory/Instructions UI elements in Stationpedia, so all 7 category types have working tooltips.

## Current State
The mod is at: `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\`

Phase 1 is complete:
- Data classes exist: `SlotDescription`, `VersionDescription`, `MemoryDescription`
- Helper methods exist: `GetSlotDescription()`, `GetVersionDescription()`, `GetMemoryDescription()`
- JSON schema supports all category types

Currently working (using `SPDALogicTooltip` component):
- Logic (LogicContents)
- LogicSlot (LogicSlotContents)
- Mode (ModeContents)
- Connection (ConnectionContents)

Need to implement:
- Slots (SlotContents) - uses `SPDASlot` prefab with `SlotTitle` text field
- Versions (StructureVersionContents) - uses `SPDAVersion` prefab with `BuildTitle` text field
- Memory/Instructions (LogicInstructions) - uses `SPDAGeneric` prefab with `Header` text field

## Tasks

### 1. Create new tooltip components
Create three new MonoBehaviour classes similar to `SPDALogicTooltip`:

**SPDASlotTooltip** - For slot items
- Extract slot name from `SPDASlot.SlotTitle.text`
- Call `StationpediaPlusMod.GetSlotDescription(deviceKey, slotName)`
- Format tooltip appropriately for slot info

**SPDAVersionTooltip** - For version/tier items
- Extract version name from `SPDAVersion.BuildTitle.text`
- Call `StationpediaPlusMod.GetVersionDescription(deviceKey, versionName)`
- Format tooltip appropriately for version info

**SPDAMemoryTooltip** - For memory instruction items
- Extract instruction name from `SPDAGeneric.Header.text`
- Call `StationpediaPlusMod.GetMemoryDescription(deviceKey, instructionName)`
- Format tooltip with opCode, parameters, and description

### 2. Add methods to attach tooltips to new categories
In `StationpediaPlusMod`, add methods:

```csharp
private int AddTooltipsToSlots(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)
{
    // Find SPDASlot components in category.Contents
    // Add SPDASlotTooltip to each
}

private int AddTooltipsToVersions(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)
{
    // Find SPDAVersion components in category.Contents
    // Add SPDAVersionTooltip to each
}

private int AddTooltipsToMemory(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)
{
    // Find SPDAGeneric components in category.Contents
    // Add SPDAMemoryTooltip to each
}
```

### 3. Update AddTooltipsToLogicItems to call new methods
Add calls to the new methods after the existing Logic/Mode/Connection calls:

```csharp
totalTooltipsAdded += AddTooltipsToSlots(universalPage.SlotContents, pageKey);
totalTooltipsAdded += AddTooltipsToVersions(universalPage.StructureVersionContents, pageKey);
totalTooltipsAdded += AddTooltipsToMemory(universalPage.LogicInstructions, pageKey);
```

## Reference: Existing SPDALogicTooltip pattern
The existing `SPDALogicTooltip` class shows the pattern:
- Implements `IPointerEnterHandler`, `IPointerExitHandler`
- Has `Initialize(deviceKey, itemName, categoryName)` method
- Uses 300ms hover delay coroutine
- Sets `StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText` and `ShowTooltip` for IMGUI rendering
- Caches tooltip text after first lookup

## Reference: Stationpedia UI prefabs
From `UniversalPage.cs`:
- `SlotContents` uses `SPDASlot` with `SlotTitle` (TextMeshProUGUI)
- `StructureVersionContents` uses `SPDAVersion` with `BuildTitle` (TextMeshProUGUI)
- `LogicInstructions` uses `SPDAGeneric` with `Header` (TextMeshProUGUI)

## Important
- Follow the same pattern as `SPDALogicTooltip` for consistency
- All three new tooltip classes should be in the same file (StationpediaPlus.cs)
- Each tooltip should have appropriate formatting for its category type
- Work autonomously and complete all tasks

When complete, report back with:
- Summary of changes made
- List of new classes/methods added
- Any issues encountered
```

```
        private int AddTooltipsToCategory(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey, string categoryName)
        {
            if (category == null || category.Contents == null)
            {
                return 0;
            }

            int added = 0;
            foreach (Transform child in category.Contents)
            {
                SPDALogic logic = child.GetComponent<SPDALogic>();
                if (logic != null && logic.InfoValue != null)
                {
                    var existingTooltip = logic.GetComponent<SPDALogicTooltip>();
                    if (existingTooltip == null)
                    {
                        string logicName = logic.InfoValue.text;
                        var tooltip = logic.gameObject.AddComponent<SPDALogicTooltip>();
                        tooltip.Initialize(pageKey, logicName, categoryName);
                        added++;
                    }
                }
            }
            return added;
        }

        private int AddTooltipsToSlots(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)
        {
            if (category == null || category.Contents == null)
            {
                return 0;
            }

            int added = 0;
            foreach (Transform child in category.Contents)
            {
                SPDASlot slot = child.GetComponent<SPDASlot>();
                if (slot != null && slot.SlotTitle != null)
                {
                    var existingTooltip = slot.GetComponent<SPDASlotTooltip>();
                    if (existingTooltip == null)
                    {
                        string slotName = slot.SlotTitle.text;
                        var tooltip = slot.gameObject.AddComponent<SPDASlotTooltip>();
                        tooltip.Initialize(pageKey, slotName);
                        added++;
                    }
                }
            }
            return added;
        }

        private int AddTooltipsToVersions(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)
        {
            if (category == null || category.Contents == null)
            {
                return 0;
            }

            int added = 0;
            foreach (Transform child in category.Contents)
            {
                SPDAVersion version = child.GetComponent<SPDAVersion>();
                if (version != null && version.BuildTitle != null)
                {
                    var existingTooltip = version.GetComponent<SPDAVersionTooltip>();
                    if (existingTooltip == null)
                    {
                        string versionName = version.BuildTitle.text;
                        var tooltip = version.gameObject.AddComponent<SPDAVersionTooltip>();
                        tooltip.Initialize(pageKey, versionName);
                        added++;
                    }
                }
            }
            return added;
        }

        private int AddTooltipsToMemory(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)
        {
            if (category == null || category.Contents == null)
            {
                return 0;
            }

            int added = 0;
            foreach (Transform child in category.Contents)
            {
                SPDAGeneric generic = child.GetComponent<SPDAGeneric>();
                if (generic != null && generic.Header != null)
                {
                    var existingTooltip = generic.GetComponent<SPDAMemoryTooltip>();
                    if (existingTooltip == null)
                    {
                        string instructionName = generic.Header.text;
                        var tooltip = generic.gameObject.AddComponent<SPDAMemoryTooltip>();
                        tooltip.Initialize(pageKey, instructionName);
                        added++;
                    }
                }
            }
            return added;
        }
```

```
    #endregion

    #region Slot Tooltip Component

    /// <summary>
    /// Tooltip component for SPDASlot items (device slots like Battery, ProgrammableChip, etc.)
    /// </summary>
    public class SPDASlotTooltip : MonoBehaviour, IPointerEnterHandler, IPointerExitHandler
    {
        private string _deviceKey;
        private string _slotName;
        private string _cachedTooltipText;
        private Coroutine _showCoroutine;
        private bool _isHovering;
        
        private const float HOVER_DELAY = 0.3f;

        public void Initialize(string deviceKey, string slotName)
        {
            _deviceKey = deviceKey;
            _slotName = slotName;
            _cachedTooltipText = null;
        }

        public void OnPointerEnter(PointerEventData eventData)
        {
            _isHovering = true;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
            _showCoroutine = StartCoroutine(ShowTooltipAfterDelay());
        }

        public void OnPointerExit(PointerEventData eventData)
        {
            _isHovering = false;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
            {
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
                _showCoroutine = null;
            }
            StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = false;
            StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = "";
        }

        private IEnumerator ShowTooltipAfterDelay()
        {
            yield return new WaitForSeconds(HOVER_DELAY);
            
            if (_isHovering)
            {
                string tooltipText = GetTooltipText();
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(tooltipText))
                {
                    StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = tooltipText;
                    StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = true;
                }
            }
        }

        private string GetTooltipText()
        {
            if (_cachedTooltipText != null)
                return _cachedTooltipText;

            string cleanName = CleanName(_slotName);
            var desc = StationpediaPlusMod.GetSlotDescription(_deviceKey, cleanName);
            
            if (desc != null)
            {
                _cachedTooltipText = FormatTooltip(cleanName, desc);
                return _cachedTooltipText;
            }
            
            _cachedTooltipText = $"<color=#00BFFF><b>{cleanName}</b></color>\n\n" +
                                 $"<color=#AAAAAA>No slot description available yet.</color>";
            return _cachedTooltipText;
        }

        private string FormatTooltip(string cleanName, SlotDescription desc)
        {
            return $"<color=#00BFFF><b>Slot: {cleanName}</b></color>\n" +
                   $"<color=#888888>─────────────────────</color>\n" +
                   $"<b>Type:</b> {desc.slotType}\n" +
                   $"<color=#888888>─────────────────────</color>\n" +
                   $"{desc.description}";
        }

        private string CleanName(string name)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name)) return name;
            return System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(name, "<[^>]+>", "").Trim();
        }

        private void OnDisable()
        {
            _isHovering = false;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
            {
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
                _showCoroutine = null;
            }
            StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = false;
            StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = "";
        }
    }

    #endregion

    #region Version Tooltip Component

    /// <summary>
    /// Tooltip component for SPDAVersion items (structure tiers like Tier One, Tier Two)
    /// </summary>
    public class SPDAVersionTooltip : MonoBehaviour, IPointerEnterHandler, IPointerExitHandler
    {
        private string _deviceKey;
        private string _versionName;
        private string _cachedTooltipText;
        private Coroutine _showCoroutine;
        private bool _isHovering;
        
        private const float HOVER_DELAY = 0.3f;

        public void Initialize(string deviceKey, string versionName)
        {
            _deviceKey = deviceKey;
            _versionName = versionName;
            _cachedTooltipText = null;
        }

        public void OnPointerEnter(PointerEventData eventData)
        {
            _isHovering = true;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
            _showCoroutine = StartCoroutine(ShowTooltipAfterDelay());
        }

        public void OnPointerExit(PointerEventData eventData)
        {
            _isHovering = false;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
            {
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
                _showCoroutine = null;
            }
            StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = false;
            StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = "";
        }

        private IEnumerator ShowTooltipAfterDelay()
        {
            yield return new WaitForSeconds(HOVER_DELAY);
            
            if (_isHovering)
            {
                string tooltipText = GetTooltipText();
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(tooltipText))
                {
                    StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = tooltipText;
                    StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = true;
                }
            }
        }

        private string GetTooltipText()
        {
            if (_cachedTooltipText != null)
                return _cachedTooltipText;

            string cleanName = CleanName(_versionName);
            var desc = StationpediaPlusMod.GetVersionDescription(_deviceKey, cleanName);
            
            if (desc != null)
            {
                _cachedTooltipText = FormatTooltip(cleanName, desc);
                return _cachedTooltipText;
            }
            
            _cachedTooltipText = $"<color=#90EE90><b>{cleanName}</b></color>\n\n" +
                                 $"<color=#AAAAAA>No version description available yet.</color>";
            return _cachedTooltipText;
        }

        private string FormatTooltip(string cleanName, VersionDescription desc)
        {
            return $"<color=#90EE90><b>Version: {cleanName}</b></color>\n" +
                   $"<color=#888888>─────────────────────</color>\n" +
                   $"{desc.description}";
        }

        private string CleanName(string name)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name)) return name;
            return System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(name, "<[^>]+>", "").Trim();
        }

        private void OnDisable()
        {
            _isHovering = false;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
            {
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
                _showCoroutine = null;
            }
            StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = false;
            StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = "";
        }
    }

    #endregion

    #region Memory Tooltip Component

    /// <summary>
    /// Tooltip component for SPDAGeneric items (memory instructions/opcodes)
    /// </summary>
    public class SPDAMemoryTooltip : MonoBehaviour, IPointerEnterHandler, IPointerExitHandler
    {
        private string _deviceKey;
        private string _instructionName;
        private string _cachedTooltipText;
        private Coroutine _showCoroutine;
        private bool _isHovering;
        
        private const float HOVER_DELAY = 0.3f;

        public void Initialize(string deviceKey, string instructionName)
        {
            _deviceKey = deviceKey;
            _instructionName = instructionName;
            _cachedTooltipText = null;
        }

        public void OnPointerEnter(PointerEventData eventData)
        {
            _isHovering = true;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
            _showCoroutine = StartCoroutine(ShowTooltipAfterDelay());
        }

        public void OnPointerExit(PointerEventData eventData)
        {
            _isHovering = false;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
            {
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
                _showCoroutine = null;
            }
            StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = false;
            StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = "";
        }

        private IEnumerator ShowTooltipAfterDelay()
        {
            yield return new WaitForSeconds(HOVER_DELAY);
            
            if (_isHovering)
            {
                string tooltipText = GetTooltipText();
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(tooltipText))
                {
                    StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = tooltipText;
                    StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = true;
                }
            }
        }

        private string GetTooltipText()
        {
            if (_cachedTooltipText != null)
                return _cachedTooltipText;

            string cleanName = CleanName(_instructionName);
            var desc = StationpediaPlusMod.GetMemoryDescription(_deviceKey, cleanName);
            
            if (desc != null)
            {
                _cachedTooltipText = FormatTooltip(cleanName, desc);
                return _cachedTooltipText;
            }
            
            _cachedTooltipText = $"<color=#FF69B4><b>{cleanName}</b></color>\n\n" +
                                 $"<color=#AAAAAA>No instruction description available yet.</color>";
            return _cachedTooltipText;
        }

        private string FormatTooltip(string cleanName, MemoryDescription desc)
        {
            string paramText = string.IsNullOrEmpty(desc.parameters) ? "none" : desc.parameters;
            return $"<color=#FF69B4><b>Instruction: {cleanName}</b></color>\n" +
                   $"<color=#888888>─────────────────────</color>\n" +
                   $"<b>OpCode:</b> {desc.opCode}\n" +
                   $"<b>Parameters:</b> {paramText}\n" +
                   $"<color=#888888>─────────────────────</color>\n" +
                   $"{desc.description}";
        }

        private string CleanName(string name)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name)) return name;
            return System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(name, "<[^>]+>", "").Trim();
        }

        private void OnDisable()
        {
            _isHovering = false;
            if (_showCoroutine != null)
            {
                StopCoroutine(_showCoroutine);
                _showCoroutine = null;
            }
            StationpediaPlusMod.ShowTooltip = false;
            StationpediaPlusMod.CurrentTooltipText = "";
        }
    }

    #endregion
}
```

- oldString:

```
    #endregion
}
```


</details></tool-use>

```
Review the Phase 2 implementation of StationpediaPlus expansion.

## Phase Objective
Hook tooltips to remaining Stationpedia categories: Slots, Versions, and Memory/Instructions.

## File Modified
`C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\StationpediaPlus.cs`

## Changes Made

1. **Updated AddTooltipsToLogicItems()** to call three new methods:
   - `AddTooltipsToSlots(universalPage.SlotContents, pageKey)`
   - `AddTooltipsToVersions(universalPage.StructureVersionContents, pageKey)`
   - `AddTooltipsToMemory(universalPage.LogicInstructions, pageKey)`

2. **Added AddTooltipsToSlots()** method:
   - Iterates through SlotContents category
   - Finds SPDASlot components
   - Extracts slot name from `SlotTitle.text`
   - Attaches SPDASlotTooltip component

3. **Added AddTooltipsToVersions()** method:
   - Iterates through StructureVersionContents category
   - Finds SPDAVersion components
   - Extracts version name from `BuildTitle.text`
   - Attaches SPDAVersionTooltip component

4. **Added AddTooltipsToMemory()** method:
   - Iterates through LogicInstructions category
   - Finds SPDAGeneric components
   - Extracts instruction name from `Header.text`
   - Attaches SPDAMemoryTooltip component

5. **Added SPDASlotTooltip class**:
   - Implements IPointerEnterHandler, IPointerExitHandler
   - Uses 300ms hover delay
   - Calls GetSlotDescription() for lookups
   - Formats tooltip with cyan color (#00BFFF) header

6. **Added SPDAVersionTooltip class**:
   - Same pattern as above
   - Calls GetVersionDescription() for lookups
   - Formats tooltip with light green color (#90EE90) header

7. **Added SPDAMemoryTooltip class**:
   - Same pattern as above
   - Calls GetMemoryDescription() for lookups
   - Formats tooltip with pink color (#FF69B4) header
   - Shows opCode and parameters in formatted output

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Code compiles without errors
- [ ] All 7 category types now have tooltip support
- [ ] Each tooltip type uses appropriate formatting
- [ ] Consistent hover delay (300ms) across all types
- [ ] Proper cleanup on disable

## Review Tasks
1. Verify code correctness
2. Check for consistency across tooltip components
3. Verify null safety
4. Check that existing Logic tooltips still work
5. Identify any issues or improvements

Return your review in this format:
- **Status**: APPROVED / NEEDS_REVISION / FAILED
- **Summary**: Brief summary of findings
- **Issues**: List any issues found (numbered)
- **Recommendations**: List any recommended improvements
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

Great news that tooltips are working! Let me address both points:

## Version Tooltips
The "Structure Versions" section only appears on devices that have multiple construction tiers. The Advanced Furnace might not display this section in the current game version - I'll need to find a device that actually shows versions to test it.

## Memory Tooltips - Per-Parameter Context

Looking at your screenshot, I see the structure - each instruction has:
- **Header**: `TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18`
- **Byte parameters**: `0-7 | OP_CODE | BYTE_8`, `8-39 | GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG | UINT_32`, etc.

**Option A: Per-parameter tooltips** - Would require finding and hooking into each individual parameter cell. This is complex because those appear to be nested UI elements.

**Option B: Comprehensive instruction tooltip** - Include all parameter descriptions in the main instruction tooltip. So when you hover anywhere on that row, you see:
```
Instruction: FilterGasNotContains
─────────────────────
OpCode: 18
Parameters: OP_CODE, GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG
─────────────────────
Filters contacts that do NOT contain specified gas types.

Byte Layout:
• 0-7 (OP_CODE): The instruction identifier (18)
• 8-39 (GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG): Bitflags for gas types to exclude
• 40-63: Unused
```

**My recommendation**: Option B is more practical and still gives users all the info they need. Each instruction's tooltip would document its full byte layout.

Which approach would you prefer? If Option B, I can update the `MemoryDescription` schema to include a `byteLayout` field that describes each parameter.
```

        private string FormatTooltip(string cleanName, MemoryDescription desc)
        {
            string paramText = string.IsNullOrEmpty(desc.parameters) ? "none" : desc.parameters;
            string tooltip = $"<color=#FF69B4><b>Instruction: {cleanName}</b></color>\n" +
                   $"<color=#888888>─────────────────────</color>\n" +
                   $"<b>OpCode:</b> {desc.opCode}\n" +
                   $"<b>Parameters:</b> {paramText}\n" +

                   $"{desc.description}";
            
            // Add byte layout if available
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(desc.byteLayout))
            {
                tooltip += $"\n<color=#888888>─────────────────────</color>\n" +
                          $"<color=#AAAAAA><b>Byte Layout:</b></color>\n{desc.byteLayout}";
            }
            
            return tooltip;
        }

```
- newString:

```
        private string CleanName(string name)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name)) return name;
            
            // Remove Unity rich text tags
            string cleaned = System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(name, "<[^>]+>", "").Trim();
            
            // Remove OP_CODE suffix (e.g., "TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18" -> "TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains")
            int opCodeIndex = cleaned.IndexOf(" OP_CODE:");
            if (opCodeIndex > 0)
            {
                cleaned = cleaned.Substring(0, opCodeIndex).Trim();
            }
            
            return cleaned;
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        private string CleanName(string name)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name)) return name;
            return System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(name, "<[^>]+>", "").Trim();
        }
```

<summary>Tool use: **create_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- content:

```
# StationpediaPlus Agent Instructions - Device Documentation

## Your Mission

You are documenting device-specific logic descriptions for the **StationpediaPlus** mod. Your output will be used to display helpful hover tooltips in the Stationpedia UI.

## Your Batch Assignment

Check your batch file (e.g., `batch-1.txt`) for the list of devices you need to document.

## Workspace Location

```
C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\
├── Assembly-CSharp/          <- Game source code (C# classes)
├── Stationeers/Stationpedia/ <- Stationpedia.json (device metadata)
└── StationpediaPlus/         <- Mod project
    ├── mod/descriptions.json <- OUTPUT: Add your entries here
    └── coordination/         <- Batch assignments
```

## Output Format

Add entries to `StationpediaPlus/mod/descriptions.json` following this schema:

```json
{
  "deviceKey": "ThingStructureActiveVent",
  "displayName": "Active Vent",
  "logicDescriptions": {
    "On": {
      "dataType": "Boolean",
      "range": "0-1",
      "description": "Enable/disable the vent. When on, actively moves atmosphere."
    },
    "Mode": {
      "dataType": "Integer",
      "range": "0-1",
      "description": "0 = Inward (pull gas in), 1 = Outward (push gas out)."
    },
    "Setting": {
      "dataType": "Float",
      "range": "0-60795 kPa",
      "description": "Target pressure in kPa. Vent stops when reached."
    }
  },
  "slotDescriptions": {
    "Slot Name": {
      "slotType": "SlotType",
      "description": "What this slot is for."
    }
  }
}
```

## Research Process

### Step 1: Find the Device Class

1. Get the `Key` from your batch (e.g., `ThingStructureActiveVent`)
2. Search for the corresponding class file in `Assembly-CSharp/`
3. The class name often matches: `ActiveVent.cs` or search for the prefab name

### Step 2: Read Stationpedia.json

Look up the device in `Stationeers/Stationpedia/Stationpedia.json`:

```json
{
  "Key": "ThingStructureActiveVent",
  "LogicInsert": [
    { "LogicAccessTypes": "ReadWrite", "LogicSlotType": "Unknown", "LogicName": "On" },
    { "LogicAccessTypes": "ReadWrite", "LogicSlotType": "Unknown", "LogicName": "Mode" },
    { "LogicAccessTypes": "ReadWrite", "LogicSlotType": "Unknown", "LogicName": "Setting" }
  ],
  "Slots": [...]
}
```

This tells you WHAT logic types exist. Now you need to document WHAT THEY DO.

### Step 3: Analyze Source Code

Look for these methods in the device class:

```csharp
// What values can be read
public override bool CanLogicRead(LogicType logicType)

// What values can be written
public override bool CanLogicWrite(LogicType logicType)

// How read values are calculated
public override double GetLogicValue(LogicType logicType)

// How write values are applied
public override void SetLogicValue(LogicType logicType, double value)

// Slot-specific logic
public override double GetSlotLogicValue(int slotIndex, LogicSlotType logicSlotType)
```

### Step 4: Trace Inheritance

Many devices inherit from base classes. Check:
- `Device` → `DevicePowered` → `DeviceCircuit`
- `DeviceAtmospherics` → `DeviceInput` → `DeviceInputOutput`

If the parent class has logic, the child inherits it.

## Description Guidelines

### BE BRIEF (1-3 sentences max)
```
✓ "Current internal temperature in Kelvin."
✓ "0 = Idle, 1 = Smelt, 2 = Alloy, 3 = Incinerate."
✗ "This property returns the current temperature of the device's internal chamber measured in Kelvin units..."
```

### Include Mode Values
```
"Mode": {
  "description": "0 = Inward (pull), 1 = Outward (push)."
}
```

### Include Units
```
"Temperature": { "range": "0+ K", "description": "Internal temp in Kelvin." }
"Pressure": { "range": "0+ kPa", "description": "Atmospheric pressure in kPa." }
"Power": { "range": "0+ W", "description": "Current power draw in watts." }
```

### Data Types
- `Boolean` → 0/1 values (On, Lock, Open, Error)
- `Integer` → Whole numbers (Mode, Quantity, SlotCount)
- `Float` → Decimal values (Temperature, Pressure, Ratio)
- `Hash` → CRC32 prefab hashes (PrefabHash, OccupantHash)

## Slots to Document

Only add `slotDescriptions` if the device has meaningful slots. Skip generic slots like "Contents" unless context-specific.

Look at the `Slots` array in Stationpedia.json:
```json
"Slots": [
  { "SlotName": "Import", "SlotType": "Import" },
  { "SlotName": "Export", "SlotType": "Export" }
]
```

## Skip These Devices

If a device has NO logic types (empty `LogicInsert`), skip it - it doesn't need documentation.

## Working Method

1. **Work autonomously** - Don't ask for confirmation
2. **Process in order** - Go through your batch list sequentially
3. **Create one big JSON array** - Compile all your devices into one output
4. **Report when done** - Give completion status at the end

## Output Template

When done, provide your entries as a JSON array:

```json
[
  { "deviceKey": "...", "displayName": "...", "logicDescriptions": {...} },
  { "deviceKey": "...", "displayName": "...", "logicDescriptions": {...} },
  ...
]
```

The conductor will merge all batches into the final `descriptions.json`.

## Common Patterns

| Device Type | Common Logic Types |
|-------------|-------------------|
| Furnaces | Temperature, Pressure, Mode, Setting, Activate |
| Vents | On, Mode, Setting, Pressure |
| Batteries | Charge, ChargeRatio, Maximum, PowerPotential |
| Sensors | On, Setting, Mode, Activate |
| Solar Panels | Horizontal, Vertical, SolarAngle, PossibleSolarAngle |
| IC Housings | On, Error, Setting, Mode + slot logic |

## Questions?

If you encounter a device you can't figure out:
1. Note it as "NEEDS_REVIEW" in your report
2. Include what you found
3. Move on to the next device

Don't get stuck - progress is more important than perfection.

---

**START YOUR BATCH NOW. Report back when complete.**

```

</details></tool-use>
```

# ⚠️ AGENTS: Save Your Work to Your Batch File

## IMPORTANT: Before you finish, save your device entries!

### Your Output File

Save your JSON array to your batch output file:

| Agent | Output File |
|-------|-------------|
| Agent 1 | `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-1-output.json` |
| Agent 2 | `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-2-output.json` |
| Agent 3 | `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-3-output.json` |
| Agent 4 | `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-4-output.json` |
| Agent 5 | `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-5-output.json` |
| Agent 6 | `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-6-output.json` |
| Agent 7 | `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-7-output.json` |
| Agent 8 | `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-8-output.json` |

### Required Format

Your output file must be a valid JSON array of device objects:

```json
[
  {
    "deviceKey": "ThingStructureActiveVent",
    "displayName": "Active Vent",
    "logicDescriptions": {
      "On": {
        "dataType": "Boolean",
        "range": "0-1",
        "description": "Enable/disable the vent."
      },
      "Mode": {
        "dataType": "Integer",
        "range": "0-1",
        "description": "0 = Inward, 1 = Outward."
      }
    }
  },
  {
    "deviceKey": "ThingStructureAirConditioner",
    "displayName": "Air Conditioner",
    "logicDescriptions": {
      "On": {
        "dataType": "Boolean",
        "range": "0-1", 
        "description": "Power state."
      }
    }
  }
]
```

### How to Save

Use the `create_file` tool to write your complete JSON array:

```
create_file(
  filePath: "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-{N}-output.json",
  content: <your JSON array>
)
```

### Checklist Before Finishing

- [ ] All devices from your batch are included
- [ ] JSON is valid (proper commas, brackets, quotes)
- [ ] Each device has `deviceKey`, `displayName`, and `logicDescriptions`
- [ ] File is saved to the correct `batch-{N}-output.json` path

```
<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="run_in_terminal"><details>

**Input:**

- command:

```
$outputPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs"
$allDevices = @()
$stats = @{}

for ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {
    $file = Join-Path $outputPath "batch-$i-output.json"
    try {
        $content = Get-Content $file -Raw -ErrorAction Stop
        $devices = $content | ConvertFrom-Json -ErrorAction Stop
        $count = if ($devices -is [array]) { $devices.Count } else { 1 }
        $stats["Batch $i"] = $count
        $allDevices += $devices
    } catch {
        $stats["Batch $i"] = "ERROR: $_"
    }
}

Write-Host "=== Batch Statistics ===" 
$stats.GetEnumerator() | Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "$($_.Key): $($_.Value) devices" }
Write-Host "`nTotal devices collected: $($allDevices.Count)"
```

- explanation: `Read all batch files and count devices from each`
- isBackground: `false`

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="run_in_terminal"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_in_terminal**</summary>

**Input:**

- command:

```
$outputPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs"
$descPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\descriptions.json"

# Read existing descriptions.json
$existing = Get-Content $descPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json

# Collect all batch devices
$allDevices = @()
for ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {
    $file = Join-Path $outputPath "batch-$i-output.json"
    $content = Get-Content $file -Raw
    $devices = $content | ConvertFrom-Json
    $allDevices += $devices
}

# Create lookup of existing devices by key
$existingKeys = @{}
foreach ($d in $existing.devices) {
    $existingKeys[$d.deviceKey] = $true
}

# Add new devices (skip duplicates)
$added = 0
$skipped = 0
foreach ($device in $allDevices) {
    if ($device.deviceKey -and -not $existingKeys[$device.deviceKey]) {
        $existing.devices += $device
        $existingKeys[$device.deviceKey] = $true
        $added++
    } else {
        $skipped++
    }
}

# Save merged file
$existing | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10 | Set-Content $descPath -Encoding UTF8

Write-Host "Added: $added new devices"
Write-Host "Skipped: $skipped (duplicates or invalid)"
Write-Host "Total in descriptions.json: $($existing.devices.Count)"
```

- explanation: `Merge all batch outputs into descriptions.json, skipping duplicates`
- isBackground: `false`

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="run_in_terminal"><details>

```
$batchPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\agent-instructions"
$outputPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs"

# Get all assigned devices from batch files
$assigned = @()
for ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {
    $file = Join-Path $batchPath "batch-$i.txt"
    $devices = Get-Content $file | Where-Object { $_.Trim() -ne "" }
    $assigned += $devices
}

# Get all documented devices from outputs
$documented = @()
for ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {
    $file = Join-Path $outputPath "batch-$i-output.json"
    $json = Get-Content $file -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
    foreach ($d in $json) {
        if ($d.deviceKey) { $documented += $d.deviceKey }
    }
}

# Find missing
$missing = $assigned | Where-Object { $_ -notin $documented }

Write-Host "Assigned: $($assigned.Count)"
Write-Host "Documented: $($documented.Count)"
Write-Host "Missing: $($missing.Count)"
Write-Host ""

# Save missing list
$missing | Out-File "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\missing-devices.txt" -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "Missing devices saved to missing-devices.txt"
```

- explanation: `Compare assigned vs documented devices to find what's missing`
- isBackground: `false`

</details></tool-use>

```
$missing = Get-Content "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\missing-devices.txt" | Where-Object { $_.Trim() -ne "" }
$stationpedia = Get-Content "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\Stationeers\Stationpedia\Stationpedia.json" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json

$withLogic = @()
$withoutLogic = @()

foreach ($key in $missing) {
    $page = $stationpedia.pages | Where-Object { $_.Key -eq $key }
    if ($page) {
        $logicCount = $page.LogicInsert.Count
        if ($logicCount -gt 0) {
            $withLogic += [PSCustomObject]@{ Key = $key; LogicCount = $logicCount }
        } else {
            $withoutLogic += $key
        }
    }
}

Write-Host "=== Missing Devices Analysis ==="
Write-Host "Missing with logic types: $($withLogic.Count)"
Write-Host "Missing without logic: $($withoutLogic.Count)"
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "First 20 devices that SHOULD have been documented (have logic):"
$withLogic | Select-Object -First 20 | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  $($_.Key) ($($_.LogicCount) logic types)" }
```

- explanation: `Check if missing devices have logic types in Stationpedia`
- isBackground: `false`

</details></tool-use>
```

$batchPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\agent-instructions"
$outputPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs"

for ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {
    $batchFile = Join-Path $batchPath "batch-$i.txt"
    $outputFile = Join-Path $outputPath "batch-$i-output.json"
    
    $assigned = Get-Content $batchFile | Where-Object { $_.Trim() -ne "" }
    $output = Get-Content $outputFile -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
    $documented = @()
    foreach ($d in $output) { if ($d.deviceKey) { $documented += $d.deviceKey } }
    
    $missing = $assigned | Where-Object { $_ -notin $documented }
    
    Write-Host "Batch $i : Assigned=$($assigned.Count) Documented=$($documented.Count) Skipped=$($missing.Count)"
    if ($missing.Count -gt 0 -and $missing.Count -le 10) {
        Write-Host "  Skipped: $($missing -join ', ')"
    } elseif ($missing.Count -gt 10) {
        Write-Host "  First skipped: $($missing[0])"
        Write-Host "  Last assigned: $($assigned[-1])"
    }
    Write-Host ""
}

$descPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\descriptions.json"
$content = Get-Content $descPath -Raw

# Use .NET JSON to reformat properly
Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Web.Extensions
$serializer = New-Object System.Web.Script.Serialization.JavaScriptSerializer
$serializer.MaxJsonLength = [int]::MaxValue

# Parse and re-serialize won't help with PowerShell, so let's use a different approach
# Read as object, write with proper formatting using .NET

$json = $content | ConvertFrom-Json

# Convert to formatted JSON using .NET
$formatted = [System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer]::Serialize($json, [System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions]@{
    WriteIndented = $true
})

$formatted | Set-Content $descPath -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "Done"
```

- explanation: `Use .NET JsonSerializer for proper formatting`
```

```
<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="run_in_terminal"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_in_terminal**</summary>

**Input:**

- command:

```
$op = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs"
$descPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\descriptions.json"

# Collect all devices
$allDevices = @()
for ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {
    $f = Join-Path $op "batch-$i-output.json"
    $content = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($f)
    $devices = $content | ConvertFrom-Json
    $allDevices += $devices
}

# Build the full structure
$result = @{
    version = "2.0.0"
    devices = $allDevices
    genericDescriptions = @{
        logic = @{
            Power = "Returns 1 if device is powered, 0 if not."
            On = "Power state. 1 = on, 0 = off."
            Error = "Returns 1 if device has an error."
            Lock = "Lock state. 1 = locked, 0 = unlocked."
            Temperature = "Temperature in Kelvin."
            Pressure = "Pressure in kPa."
            RequiredPower = "Power draw in watts."
            ReferenceId = "Unique device identifier."
            Setting = "Configurable device value."
        }
        slots = @{
            Battery = "Slot for battery cells to power the device."
            ProgrammableChip = "IC10 chip slot for automation scripts."
            GasCanister = "Slot for gas canisters."
            Import = "Input slot for items entering the device."
            Export = "Output slot for processed items."
        }
        versions = @{
            "Tier One" = "Basic version with standard efficiency."
            "Tier Two" = "Improved version with better efficiency."
        }
        memory = @{
            StackPointer = "Current stack pointer position in device memory."
            ExecuteRecipe = "Triggers execution of a crafting recipe."
        }
    }
}

# Convert to JSON with proper formatting using .NET
$jsonString = $result | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10

# Write with UTF8 no BOM
$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($descPath, $jsonString, $utf8NoBom)

Write-Host "Created descriptions.json with $($allDevices.Count) devices"
```

- explanation: `Rebuild descriptions.json properly from batch outputs`
- isBackground: `false`

</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="run_in_terminal"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_in_terminal**</summary>

**Input:**

- command:

```
$op = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs"
$descPath = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\descriptions.json"

# Read each batch and extract just the array content (without outer brackets)
$deviceEntries = @()
for ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {
    $f = Join-Path $op "batch-$i-output.json"
    $content = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($f).Trim()
    # Remove outer [ and ]
    $inner = $content.Substring(1, $content.Length - 2).Trim()
    if ($inner.Length -gt 0) {
        $deviceEntries += $inner
    }
}

# Build the final JSON manually
$devicesJson = $deviceEntries -join ",`n  "

$finalJson = @"
{
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "devices": [
  $devicesJson
  ],
  "genericDescriptions": {
    "logic": {
      "Power": "Returns 1 if device is powered, 0 if not.",
      "On": "Power state. 1 = on, 0 = off.",
      "Error": "Returns 1 if device has an error.",
      "Lock": "Lock state. 1 = locked, 0 = unlocked.",
      "Temperature": "Temperature in Kelvin.",
      "Pressure": "Pressure in kPa.",
      "RequiredPower": "Power draw in watts.",
      "ReferenceId": "Unique device identifier.",
      "Setting": "Configurable device value."
    },
    "slots": {
      "Battery": "Slot for battery cells to power the device.",
      "ProgrammableChip": "IC10 chip slot for automation scripts.",
      "GasCanister": "Slot for gas canisters.",
      "Import": "Input slot for items entering the device.",
      "Export": "Output slot for processed items."
    },
    "versions": {
      "Tier One": "Basic version with standard efficiency.",
      "Tier Two": "Improved version with better efficiency."
    },
    "memory": {
      "StackPointer": "Current stack pointer position in device memory.",
      "ExecuteRecipe": "Triggers execution of a crafting recipe."
    }
  }
}
"@

$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($descPath, $finalJson, $utf8NoBom)
Write-Host "Created descriptions.json"
```

- explanation: `Manually concatenate batch files to preserve their formatting`
- isBackground: `false`

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_
```

```
<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="create_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- content:

```
# Agent Review Instructions: Verify Device-Specific Logic Types

## Your Mission

Your previous batch has descriptions that are mostly correct for generic types, but the **device-specific** logic types need verification against the actual decompiled game code. You must READ THE SOURCE CODE for each device and fix any incorrect descriptions.

## Logic Types That MUST Be Verified

These types have **different meanings per device** - you CANNOT guess:

| Logic Type | Why It Varies |
|------------|---------------|
| `Mode` | Different enums per device (BatteryCellState, VentMode, FurnaceMode, etc.) |
| `Setting` | Device-specific purpose (temperature, pressure, ratio, watts, etc.) |
| `Ratio` | What ratio? (charge ratio, fill ratio, progress ratio, etc.) |
| `Error` | What conditions trigger error? |
| `Lock` | What does locking do for THIS device? |
| `Activate` | What does activation trigger? |
| `Output` / `Output2` | What value is being output? |
| `Input` / `Input2` | What value is being input? |
| `CombustionInput` / `CombustionInput2` / `CombustionOutput` / `CombustionOutput2` | Combustion-specific values |

## Verification Process

### Step 1: Find Your Batch Output File

Your batch output is at:
```
C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs\batch-{N}-output.json
```

### Step 2: For Each Device, Find the Source Class

1. Take the deviceKey (e.g., `ThingStructureBatteryLarge`)
2. Search for the class in `Assembly-CSharp/` folder
3. The class is usually named like `Battery.cs`, `ActiveVent.cs`, etc.

### Step 3: Find These Methods in Source Code

```csharp
// Check what Mode values mean
public override string[] ModeStrings { get; }  // Often references an enum

// Check how values are read
public override double GetLogicValue(LogicType logicType)
{
    if (logicType == LogicType.Mode)
        return (double)this._someState;  // <-- What is this?
}

// Check what Setting does
public override void SetLogicValue(LogicType logicType, double value)
{
    if (logicType == LogicType.Setting)
        this.TargetPressure = value;  // <-- What property does it set?
}
```

### Step 4: Look Up Enums

If Mode uses an enum, find it:
```csharp
// Example: Battery uses BatteryCellState
public enum BatteryCellState { Empty, Critical, VeryLow, Low, Medium, High, Full }
```

### Step 5: Update Your Descriptions

Fix any wrong descriptions. Example correction:

**WRONG (hallucinated):**
```json
"Mode": {
  "description": "0 = Auto, 1 = Prioritize Charging."
}
```

**CORRECT (from source code):**
```json
"Mode": {
  "description": "Read-only charge state: 0=Empty, 1=Critical, 2=VeryLow, 3=Low, 4=Medium, 5=High, 6=Full."
}
```

## Red Flags - Descriptions That Are Probably Wrong

Watch for these patterns that suggest hallucination:

- "0 = Auto, 1 = Manual" (generic guess)
- "0 = Off, 1 = On" for Mode (that's `On`, not `Mode`)
- "Target value" without saying target of WHAT
- "Sets the device setting" (circular, meaningless)
- Any Mode description with only 2 values (most have 3+)

## Output Format

Create an **amendments file** with ONLY the devices that needed fixes:

```json
[
  {
    "deviceKey": "ThingStructureBatteryLarge",
    "fixes": {
      "Mode": {
        "dataType": "Integer",
        "range": "0-6",
        "description": "Read-only charge state: 0=Empty, 1=Critical, 2=VeryLow, 3=Low, 4=Medium, 5=High, 6=Full."
      }
    },
    "notes": "Mode is read-only (CanLogicWrite returns false for Mode). Values from BatteryCellState enum."
  },
  {
    "deviceKey": "ThingStructureActiveVent",
    "fixes": {
      "Mode": {
        "dataType": "Integer",
        "range": "0-1",
        "description": "0=Inward (pull atmosphere in), 1=Outward (push atmosphere out)."
      }
    },
    "notes": "Verified from ActiveVent.cs ModeStrings array."
  }
]
```

Save to: `coordination/outputs/batch-{N}-amendments.json`

## Devices to Prioritize

Focus on devices with these characteristics:
1. Has `Mode` logic type - ALWAYS verify
2. Has `Setting` that isn't obvious (not just On/Off)
3. Has `Ratio`, `Output`, `Input` types
4. Complex devices: Furnaces, Fabricators, IC Housings, Sensors

## What NOT to Change

These are usually correct and don't need verification:
- `Power` - Always 0/1 powered state
- `On` - Always 0/1 on/off state (unless device is special)
- `RequiredPower` - Always watts
- `Temperature` - Always Kelvin
- `Pressure` - Always kPa
- `ReferenceId` - Always unique ID
- `PrefabHash` - Always CRC32 hash

## Example Verification Workflow

### Device: ThingStructureBatteryLarge

1. **Find source**: `Assembly-CSharp/Assets/Scripts/Objects/Electrical/Battery.cs`

2. **Check Mode**:
```csharp
public override string[] ModeStrings => Battery.BatteryModeStrings;
public static string[] BatteryModeStrings = Enum.GetNames(typeof(BatteryCellState));
```

3. **Find enum**:
```csharp
public enum BatteryCellState { Empty, Critical, VeryLow=2, Low=3, Medium, High, Full }
```

4. **Check if writable**:
```csharp
public override bool CanLogicWrite(LogicType logicType)
{
    return logicType != LogicType.Mode && base.CanLogicWrite(logicType);  // MODE IS READ-ONLY!
}
```

5. **Write accurate description**:
```json
"Mode": {
  "dataType": "Integer", 
  "range": "0-6",
  "description": "Read-only charge state: 0=Empty, 1=Critical, 2=VeryLow, 3=Low, 4=Medium, 5=High, 6=Full."
}
```

## Completion Report

After reviewing your batch, report:
```
BATCH {N} REVIEW COMPLETE
-------------------------
Devices reviewed: {count}
Devices amended: {count}
Devices correct (no changes): {count}

Key fixes made:
- ThingStructureBatteryLarge: Mode was wrong (said Auto/Charging, actually charge state)
- ThingStructureActiveVent: Mode verified correct
- ...

Amendments saved to: coordination/outputs/batch-{N}-amendments.json
```

---

## Your Assignment

1. Load your original batch output
2. For each device with Mode/Setting/Ratio/etc., find the source file
3. Verify the description matches what the code actually does
4. Create amendments file with fixes
5. Report back with summary

**Work autonomously. Read the actual source code. No guessing.**

```

- filePath: `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\agent-instructions\REVIEW-INSTRUCTIONS.md`

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="run_in_terminal"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_in_terminal**</summary>

**Input:**

- command:

```
$problematicTypes = @('Mode', 'Setting', 'Ratio', 'Error', 'Lock', 'Activate', 'Output', 'Output2', 'Input', 'Input2', 'CombustionInput', 'CombustionInput2', 'CombustionOutput', 'CombustionOutput2')
$op = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs"

for ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {
    $file = Join-Path $op "batch-$i-output.json"
    $devices = Get-Content $file -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
    $needsReview = 0
    foreach ($d in $devices) {
        if ($d.logicDescriptions) {
            $props = $d.logicDescriptions.PSObject.Properties.Name
            $hasProblematic = $props | Where-Object { $_ -in $problematicTypes }
            if ($hasProblematic) { $needsReview++ }
        }
    }
    Write-Host "Batch $i : $($devices.Count) devices, $needsReview need review"
}
```

- explanation: `Count how many devices per batch have logic types that need verification`
- isBackground: `false`
```

```
_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="run_in_terminal"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_in_terminal**</summary>

**Input:**

- command:

```
$stationpedia = Get-Content "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\Stationeers\Stationpedia\Stationpedia.json" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
$descriptions = Get-Content "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\descriptions.json" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json

$missingCount = 0
$totalMissing = 0

foreach ($device in $descriptions.devices) {
    $spPage = $stationpedia.pages | Where-Object { $_.Key -eq $device.deviceKey }
    if ($spPage -and $spPage.LogicInsert) {
        $spLogicTypes = $spPage.LogicInsert | ForEach-Object { $_.LogicName -replace 'LogicType', '' }
        $docLogicTypes = if ($device.logicDescriptions) { $device.logicDescriptions.PSObject.Properties.Name } else { @() }
        $missing = $spLogicTypes | Where-Object { $_ -notin $docLogicTypes }
        if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {
            $missingCount++
            $totalMissing += $missing.Count
        }
    }
}

Write-Host "Devices with missing logic types: $missingCount"
Write-Host "Total missing logic type entries: $totalMissing"
```

- explanation: `Check how many devices have missing logic types compared to Stationpedia`
- isBackground: `false`

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="create_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- content:

```
$stationpedia = Get-Content "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\Stationeers\Stationpedia\Stationpedia.json" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
$descriptions = Get-Content "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\mod\descriptions.json" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
$op = "C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\outputs"

$allMissing = @()

foreach ($device in $descriptions.devices) {
    $spPage = $stationpedia.pages | Where-Object { $_.Key -eq $device.deviceKey }
    if ($spPage -and $spPage.LogicInsert) {
        $spLogicTypes = $spPage.LogicInsert | ForEach-Object { $_.LogicName -replace 'LogicType', '' }
        $docLogicTypes = if ($device.logicDescriptions) { $device.logicDescriptions.PSObject.Properties.Name } else { @() }
        $missing = $spLogicTypes | Where-Object { $_ -notin $docLogicTypes }
        if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {
            $allMissing += [PSCustomObject]@{
                deviceKey = $device.deviceKey
                missingTypes = ($missing -join ", ")
                missingCount = $missing.Count
            }
        }
    }
}

Write-Host "Devices with missing logic types: $($allMissing.Count)"
Write-Host "Total missing entries: $(($allMissing | Measure-Object -Property missingCount -Sum).Sum)"
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Top 10 devices with most missing:"
$allMissing | Sort-Object -Property missingCount -Descending | Select-Object -First 10 | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "  $($_.deviceKey): $($_.missingCount) missing - $($_.missingTypes)"
}

$allMissing | ConvertTo-Json | Out-File (Join-Path $op "missing-logic-types.json") -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Full list saved to: missing-logic-types.json"

```

- filePath: `C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\check-missing-types.ps1`

</details></tool-use>
```
